evals.prompt: How do I unit test a NestJS service that depends on PrismaService?
evals.expected_output: Use Test.createTestingModule({ providers: [UsersService, { provide: PrismaService, useValue: { user: { findUnique: jest.fn(), create: jest.fn() } } }] }).compile(). Get the service and mock with module.get(). Use prisma.user.findUnique.mockResolvedValue(mockData) to control what the mock returns. Test both success and error paths (e.g. when findUnique returns null, expect NotFoundException).

evals.prompt: How do I write an E2E test for a NestJS REST endpoint?
evals.expected_output: Use Test.createTestingModule({ imports: [AppModule] }).compile(), create the app with module.createNestApplication(), apply the same global pipes as production (app.useGlobalPipes(new ValidationPipe(...))), and call await app.init(). Use supertest: request(app.getHttpServer()).post('/users').send(dto).expect(201). Call app.close() in afterAll.

evals.prompt: How do I bypass authentication guards in NestJS E2E tests?
evals.expected_output: Use overrideGuard() in the test module builder: Test.createTestingModule({ imports: [AppModule] }).overrideGuard(JwtAuthGuard).useValue({ canActivate: () => true }).compile(). This replaces the real guard with one that always returns true. Call this before .compile() — you can chain multiple overrideGuard() calls.
